page: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. While single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is categorized in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the middle 50%. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific vector and cryptographic operations, with Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests scoring in the top 25%. Ray tracing performance is also strong, whereas text processing and image processing remain average.

The memory subsystem delivers average read and write bandwidth, but Passmark memory and database operations score in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation on small to medium models are average, though text generation on large 70B models drops to the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, batch processing, distributed analytics, and database operations that benefit from high core counts but do not require local NVMe storage or high single-core frequency.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between poor single-core stress-ng results and strong multi-core Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption scores. Memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression are average, while database operations perform in the top 25%. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded compute tasks, batch processing, and database workloads that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic benchmark performance.

tagline: A 64-vCPU compute-optimized instance with dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
